betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Plutonium-239§f

§aThe most important plutonium isotope — best for nuclear weapons and high-strength fuels!§f

§bPlutonium-239§f is the most valuable plutonium isotope. It's a neutron capture product of §bU-238§f in nuclear reactors and is essential for both weapons and advanced fuel.

§6Properties§f:
§b• Radioactivity§f: §e5.0§f RAD/s
§b• Proportion§f: §e44.4%§f of raw plutonium
§b• Category§f: Fissile

§6Production§f:
§b• Neutron Capture§f: U-238 in nuclear reactors → Pu-239
§b• Often found§f mixed with Pu-240 (detrimental for weapons)

§6Uses§f:
§b• Plutonium Fuel§f: reactor fuel rods
§b• MOX Fuel§f: mixed with uranium fuel
§b• Nuclear Weapons§f: all (thermo)nuclear warheads using plutonium cores
§b• Mini-Nuke Weapons§f: uses nuggets
§b• Custom Nuke§f: contributes §e25§f (impressive!) to nuclear stage

§6Breeding§f:
§b• Pu-239 → Pu-240§f (disappointing isotope)

§eTip§f: Pu-239 is the gold standard for nuclear weapons. If you need pure Pu-239, avoid mixing with Pu-240 by using SILEX separation. Pure Pu-239 is essential for high-yield warheads!
